title: Roku Q4 Earnings Preview: Key Takeaways
text: Roku was scheduled to report fiscal 2021 Q4 earnings on February 16, 2022. Wall Street analysts anticipated revenue of $896.88 million and earnings per share of $0.07—representing a 38% revenue increase but an 85.71% EPS decline year-over-year. The primary concern for investors centers on global supply chain disruptions. As management noted, "the slowdown in active account growth rate this quarter was, in large part, attributable to global supply chain disruptions." Roku's account growth slowed to just 1.3 million new users in Q3—the slowest pace since the pandemic began. Roku operates two segments: players (physical devices) and platform (advertising and subscription revenue). While the platform generates most profits ($378 million gross profit in Q3), players are essential for customer acquisition. To maintain device sales amid inflation, Roku absorbed higher production costs rather than raising consumer prices. The company faced margin compression and slowed growth. Stock had declined 52.7% in the previous three months. Investors were advised to seek "a definitive timeline of when supply chain issues will ease" before considering Roku an attractive investment opportunity.
